title: Episode 16 and the Archives of Evangelion set
display_title: Episode 16 and the Archives of Evangelion set
wikitext:
[]: Episode 16 and the Archives of Evangelion set             Quote Post by ruboline » Tue Mar 05, 2019 10:36 am So, as far as i understand, the AoE boxset consists of original broadcast editions of the 26 episodes - would this not be a basis for the color-correction of ep. 16? Is this version used as a basis for the color-correcting of that episode? If not, why? Is the broadcast quality not true to the original masters, since it was broadcast on TV? Sorry for the possible newbie questions.

[]: Re: Episode 16 and the Archives of Evangelion set             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Mar 05, 2019 12:07 pm Given the loss of original materials for that episode, together with the unfortunately poor surviving interpositive that was used for the Renewal remastering, the broadcast version is the best source for colour and contrast matching; but its resolution is poorer than the Renewal version - which is why that was used as the source of episode 16 for the Bluray edition (the rest were newly scanned). Sephirotic (of this forum, but not around for a long time) spent a lot of effort improving the colour of a BD rip using the archive version as reference. In fact, he went through the whole series doing this. He wrote this article about the process. Whether his judgement is better than that of the people who made the BDs for Khara is a reasonable question, of course, and not one I can answer; but I find his arguments persuasive. That article is about episode 19, but he wrote about his work on 16 specifically in detail here and it's mentioned in this forum here .

[]: Re: Episode 16 and the Archives of Evangelion set             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Mar 05, 2019 12:46 pm Wasn't aware of those BD issues. I will have to seeit in movement. That's the version coming to Netflix?

[]: Re: Episode 16 and the Archives of Evangelion set             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Mar 05, 2019 1:07 pm pwhodges wrote: ...the unfortunately poor surviving interpositive that was used for the Renewal remastering...

[]: Re: Episode 16 and the Archives of Evangelion set             Quote Post by ruboline » Tue Mar 05, 2019 1:57 pm pwhodges wrote: Given the loss of original materials for that episode, together with the unfortunately poor surviving interpositive that was used for the Renewal remastering, the broadcast version is the best source for colour and contrast matching; but its resolution is poorer than the Renewal version - which is why that was used as the source for the Bluray edition. Sephirotic (of this forum, but not around for a long time) spent a lot of effort improving the colour of a BD rip using the archive version as reference. In fact, he went through the whole series doing this. He wrote this article about the process. Whether his judgement is better than that of the people who made the BDs for Khara is a reasonable question, of course, and not one I can answer; but I find his arguments persuasive. That article is about episode 19, but he wrote about his work on 16 specifically in detail here .

[]: Re: Episode 16 and the Archives of Evangelion set             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Mar 05, 2019 7:43 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Minor correction: According to the Platinum booklets, which should be a translation from Renewal, it was an internegative .

[]: Re: Episode 16 and the Archives of Evangelion set             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Mar 05, 2019 7:47 pm ruboline wrote: Now, another (perhaps related) question - is the Genesis laserdisc release 0:X also direct broadcast versions? Of course, only 21-24 are present there, but that seems like it would be an ideal blueprint for color-correction of later releases, or am i wrong? Does the 0:X differ from the source material, and that's why i don't recall seeing it in use in this context?

[]: Re: Episode 16 and the Archives of Evangelion set             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Mar 05, 2019 7:50 pm kuribo-04 wrote: Wasn't aware of those BD issues. I will have to seeit in movement. That's the version coming to Netflix?

[]: Re: Episode 16 and the Archives of Evangelion set             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Thu Feb 09, 2023 11:20 am Apologies. As a friendly correction, if you're referring to BD-J, that is incorrect. BD-J uses a different image than that of Renewal. In possession of all 4 copies, those being the '97 Jewel DVD, 2003's Renewal, and the 2015 Blu-ray & Archival box. Now the Japanese Blu-ray box Macross Plus for example, that indeed uses the 2007 remaster, albeit in a bigger space. Below are a few examples taken directly from DVD (& Blu-ray), with one or two caveats. While playback's not ideal (computer/PowerDVD14 using an external Blu-ray disc drive), it's nonetheless accomplished using a retail disc (DVD; Blu-ray a 1:1 copy due to DRM) and software that's the next best thing; images weren't gathered using a t.v. and tabletop Blu-ray player. That of course would be perfect. But yeah. With regard to DVD however, I'm unable to accurately capture images using snapshot; despite step-by-step play, image produced via Snapshot is not what appears on-screen. I'm assuming playback can be manipulated like it can Blu-ray in these moments, but there you go. Instead, I use Windows Key+PrtScr, resizing as close to the original as PHOTOS/COMPUTER will allow (853x480). Blu-ray on the other hand remains as is, despite needing a slight bump up (1934x1088). And while the opposite's true, despite both instances lacking a disc (thumb drive), Windows Key+PrtScr produces a sharper result than that of playback software. '97 Jewel DVD '03 Renewal '15 Archival Box '15 Blu-ray Box '97 Jewel DVD '03 Renewal '15 Archival Box '15 Blu-ray Box ' 97 Jewel DVD ' 03 Renewal ' 15 Archival Box '15 Blu-ray Box
